_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize IL federal batch: IL-05 Quigley, IL-06 Casten, IL-07 Davis (retiring), IL-09 Schakowsky (retiring), IL-10 Schneider. All TRUE/FALSE cite House Clerk roll-call XML (spot-re-verified directly this pass for rolls 295-2021, 029-2023, 410-2022, 455-2002), cosponsorships (congress.gov/GovTrack — Schakowsky HR7555 cosponsorship re-verified on GovTrack), the member's own official statements/pages, or verified endorsements. Nulls = no evidence found; never inferred from party.

mike-quigley.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Straight party-line progressive on every marquee social/border/gun/election roll call checked (WHPA Yea, Born-Alive Nay, RFMA Yea x2, Equality Act Yea, H.R.2 Nay, SAVE Nay x2, AWB Yea, BSCA Yea, pistol-brace CRA Nay, CBDC Act No). Former LGBT Equality Caucus vice-chair; Gun Violence Prevention Task Force vice-chair; co-chairs the Congressional Ukraine Caucus. Deviations: authored bipartisan 'Reinventing Government' budget reports proposing ~$2T/10yr in savings + budget-process reform bill with Rep. Renacci (R); first prominent IL Democrat to call on Biden to withdraw (2024-07-05); AUMF-repeal Yea. On the 2026-03-17 Dem primary ballot for reelection AND announced (2026-01-06) he will run for Chicago mayor in 2027 — formal mayoral announcement expected after the primary.

0.note: Voted Yea on Women's Health Protection Act (roll 295, 2021); office 'choice' page champions abortion access and Title X/Planned Parenthood funding from his Appropriations seat.

1.note: WHPA Yea (2021); Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act Nay (roll 29, 2023); cosponsored EACH Act to repeal the Hyde Amendment.

2.note: No stem-cell/IVF roll call or statement found (entered House 2009, after the 2005/2007 votes).

4.note: Abortion-industry PAC receipts not itemized this pass (OpenSecrets/FEC blocked); longtime Planned Parenthood funding advocate but no funding record verified.

sean-casten.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Uniform progressive record (Heritage Action 0% in 116th and 117th, HRC 100): WHPA Yea, Born-Alive Nay, RFMA Yea x2, Equality Act Yea, trans-sports Nay x2 (2023, 2025), H.R.2 Nay, Laken Riley Nay, SAVE Nay x2, AWB Yea, BSCA Yea, IVF-right cosponsor, vaccine-mandate-repeal No x2. Openly secular: Congressional Freethought Caucus member; 2020 tweet mocking Sec. Pompeo's public Bible-verse post ('Scoundrels hide behind religion...'). Deviations: prominent crypto skeptic (FIT21 No against 71 Dem yes votes; Stand With Crypto 'strongly against'); Aye on the 2023 Fiscal Responsibility Act spending-caps deal; returned ~$50k to AIPAC-affiliated PAC donors and aligns with J Street; AUMF-repeal Yea. Won the 2026-03-17 Dem primary; faces R Niki Conforti in the Nov 3 general.

1.note: WHPA Yea (2021); Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act Nay (roll 29, 2023).

2.note: Cosponsored the Access to Family Building Act creating a federal IVF right (2024) — documented contrary to opposing IVF embryo discard.

3.note: No evidence found on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: PP Action Fund endorsement documented, but the cell concerns PAC funding; itemized receipts not retrievable this pass (OpenSecrets blocked). EMILY's List endorsed his 2022 primary opponent.

danny-davis.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Retiring: announced 2025-07-31 he will not seek reelection (age 85); serving out the term to Jan 2027; endorsed state Rep. La Shawn Ford as successor. 29-year progressive record with REAL deviations: voted YEA on the 2011 SOAR Act (D.C. Opportunity Scholarship vouchers — one of few Democrats); AUTHORED the Second Chance Act (signed by President George W. Bush, 2008, 26 R cosponsors); long bipartisan adoption/foster portfolio (Adoption Tax Credit Refundability Act with Franks/Black/Aderholt/Bacon/Feenstra; CCAI adoption-caucus co-chair); voted NAY on the 2002 Iraq AUMF; Baptist deacon with 100+ faith-leader campaign endorsements; was 'Not Voting' on the Federal Marriage Amendment both times (2004, 2006) rather than voting No. Otherwise party-line: no pro-life votes found across 1997-2023 (partial-birth ban No x3, WHPA Yea, Born-Alive Nay, embryonic stem-cell Yea x2, assisted-suicide-ban No), RFMA Yea x2, Equality Act Yea, HRC 100, H.R.2 Nay, SAVE Nay x2, AWB/BSCA Yea, defends Chicago sanctuary status. Flag: attended Scientology's 2012 DC office opening and defended the group ('I don't believe any of these people are kooks') per Washingtonian.

jan-schakowsky.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Retiring: announced 2025-05-05 she will not seek reelection in 2026 (own press release); serving out her 14th term as Chief Deputy Whip. Progressive standard-bearer (Heritage Action 0%, HRC-profile 100, NRA F): WHPA original cosponsor ('the most fundamental of rights'), EACH Act lead (Hyde repeal), Born-Alive Nay, embryonic stem-cell Yea x2, RFMA Yea x2 + FMA Nay x2, Equality Act Yea (2019, 2021), Conscience Protection Act Nay (2016), DC-vouchers No (2011), H.R.2/SAVE/Laken Riley Nay, defends sanctuary ('federal agents abducting our neighbors'), AWB/BSCA Yea, voted against the 2023 debt deal from the left. REAL cross-party deviations on war powers + Pentagon accountability: NAY on the 2002 Iraq AUMF, Yea on its 2021 repeal, championed the Yemen War Powers Resolution, and cosponsors the Audit the Pentagon Act of 2026 (H.R.7555, verified on GovTrack); career anti-Big-Pharma drug-pricing portfolio including bipartisan bills with R's Braun/Rooney/Westerman; bipartisan ADPPA data-privacy work with Bilirakis. J Street-aligned — publicly retracted an endorsement in Feb 2026 over the candidate's AIPAC funding ('leaders who put voters first, not AIPAC').

brad-schneider.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. New Democrat Coalition chair (since Jan 2025); stayed in the House race (did NOT enter the open Durbin Senate race) — won the 2026-03-17 Dem primary, on the Nov 3 general ballot. Party-line on every marquee social/border/gun/election vote checked (WHPA Yea + 'I am 100% pro-choice and I will always stand with Planned Parenthood', Born-Alive Nay, RFMA Yea x2, Equality Act Yea, HRC 100, H.R.2 Nay, Laken Riley Nay (missed final vote, said he'd have voted no), SAVE Nay x2, AWB/BSCA Yea, pistol-brace CRA Nay, NARAL endorsed). Centrist deviations: Aye on FIT21 crypto framework (2024); Aye on the 2023 Fiscal Responsibility Act; AUMF-repeal Yea. Marquee foreign-lobby alignment: displays AIPAC/DMFI/JDCA endorsements on his own campaign site, included in AIPAC's first 2024 endorsement round, sponsored the anti-BDS H.Res.246 (passed 398-17), publicly defends AIPAC; itemized AIPAC-earmarked contribution totals were not retrievable this pass.
